title: 'confidence' and 'ENGAGES' speak in different voices 1.5rem apart in the same rail
problem: The same Fira Code labelling voice is set five ways: .fe-label is uppercase with .1em tracking (lines 307–310) while .f-conf directly beneath it in the same rail stack is lowercase with no tracking (line 314); masthead labels are lowercase .08em (line 210), the pill eyebrow and toggle uppercase .12em (lines 174, 182), and .dc-label .04em (line 393). The rail clash ('ENGAGES … confidence 88') is unmistakable once seen, on every card in both themes. The stylesheet defines tracking tokens for small-caps (lines 29–30) but none for the mono voice.
recommendation: Add a --track-mono token (.1em) and give .f-conf the .fe-label treatment (uppercase, .68rem, .1em) so the rail speaks with one voice — pure CSS, since the bold number re-declares its own size/tracking (line 315) and digits are unaffected by text-transform. Align .dc-label and .mm-label to the same token (the lowercase-keys vs uppercase-headings case split is fine if consistent).
evidence: before-finding-card-dark.png / before-finding-card-light.png ('ENGAGES' tracked vs 'confidence' untracked); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 174, 182, 209–212, 307–315, 392–393
reasons: taste: Y — 'ENGAGES' and 'confidence' speaking in different voices 1.5rem apart in the same rail is unmistakable once seen; one --track-mono token is exactly how this stylesheet already thinks.
risk: Y — Pure CSS; the bold value re-declares size/tracking at line 315 and digits are immune to text-transform, so only the label's voice changes as intended.
value: Y — Verified: 'ENGAGES' and 'confidence' speak in two voices 1.5rem apart on every single card, and the fix is pure CSS with a reusable token.

title: The em-dash in the rail ENGAGES list wraps inconsistently — bind it to the relation word
problem: The dash lives in the breakable principle text node ('shortName + " — "', lines 916–917) while only the relation span is nowrap (line 312), so line breaks fall on either side of it. Both failure modes appear in the shipped screenshots: 'Integral development / — violates' (dash opens line 2) and 'Social justice — / violates' (dash dangles at line end, before-boundary-dark.png). One list, two wrap grammars.
recommendation: Move the dash into the no-wrap relation group so a wrap always reads 'Principle / — relation', never 'Principle — / relation'. Optionally add a small hanging indent so wrapped relation lines read as subordinate.
evidence: before-finding-card-dark.png, before-finding-card-light.png, before-boundary-dark.png (both wrap patterns); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 311–313, 916–917
reasons: taste: Y — Two wrap grammars in one list — dash opening one line, dangling at another — is micro-typographic noise; binding the dash to the relation word makes every wrap read identically.

risk: Y — Safe textContent-only restructure, but scope the optional .65rem hanging indent to desktop — it conflicts with C58's mobile display:contents row where the negative text-indent reads as a stray left shift.
value: Y — Both wrap failures ('— violates' opening a line, dash dangling) are visible in shipped screenshots on the element repeated beside every finding.

title: Mobile rail: the confidence number stacks on its own line inside the inline meta strip
problem: Desktop sets .f-rail .f-conf b{display:block} (line 315) so the number sits under its label in the vertical rail; the ≤680px block (lines 431–432) flips the rail to a horizontal wrapped row but never overrides the block display, so the inline strip fractures — measured at 375px the conf box is 37px tall with 'confidence' and '88' stacked, inside what should be one quiet inline run before the title.
recommendation: In the mobile media query, render the value inline after its label.
evidence: /tmp/mh-mobile/mob-frail.png; measured conf box h=37 with b at y+17; final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 314–315 vs 414–436
reasons: taste: Y — A 37px-tall stacked fragment inside what should be one quiet inline strip is visibly unfinished on mobile; one declaration fixes it.
risk: Y — One declaration inside the existing 680px block; no desktop or print exposure.
value: Y — A 37px-tall stacked fragment inside what should be one quiet inline strip is visible breakage on every mobile finding, fixed by one declaration.

title: Mobile rail: let the ✴ share the ENGAGES line instead of sitting alone
problem: In the ≤680px rail row, .f-engages is a single flex item whose max-content width forces it to wrap below the star, so the ✴ opens the finding as a stray lone glyph line above a 2–3 line meta strip (measured star at y=4522 alone, ENGAGES label at y=4549.8) — four lines of marginalia before the title.
recommendation: Use display:contents on .f-engages in the mobile query so the label and items become flex items of .f-rail and flow inline after the star.
reasons: taste: Y — A stray lone glyph line above the mobile meta strip is visible debris; display:contents on a div of styled spans is safe here since the element carries no semantics to flatten.

risk: Y — display:contents on semantics-free spans is safe in all current engines, but flag the interaction with C18: its hanging text-indent on fe-items must be reset (or desktop-scoped) in this mobile row or first lines shift left oddly.
value: Y — The lone ✴ line on mobile is mildly awkward rather than broken; display:contents is fine on these plain divs and costs one rule.

title: Finding IDs are displayed nowhere, leaving 'reason merged into F1' a dangling, unlinked cross-reference
problem: Findings carry stable ids (anchors #f-F1…, line 902) but the ID is never rendered — the rail shows only star/engages/confidence (lines 904–928). Yet the 'Dropped and consolidated' closing prints 'reason merged into F1' as plain text (fmtReason, lines 1025–1028; visible in before-footer-dark.png), a reference to a label that does not exist anywhere on the page and is not a link. A reader cannot resolve which finding 'F1' is, and the flat list of near-identical cards gives no citable identity or positional sense.
recommendation: Render the finding's ID as a small Fira Code label beside the rail ✴ — same quiet mono voice as the confidence number — so every card has a visible, citable identity. Then make 'merged into F1' a real anchor to #f-F1 (only when the target exists). Pairs with C24's permalink/:target work.
evidence: before-footer-dark.png / before-closings-dark.png ('reason merged / into F1'); final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 902, 904–928, 1025–1028, 1111–1112
amendments: Coordinate with C24 — the railhead wrapper and the star-as-link must be built in one pass.
Coordinate the rail-head markup with C24's permalink star so the two changes share one structure.
reasons: taste: Y — 'Merged into F1' referencing a label that exists nowhere is a broken promise to the reader; a quiet mono ID in the rail is information, not decoration, for an artifact meant to be cited.

risk: Y — Renders via el()/textContent with a getElementById existence guard, findings render before the dropped list so the anchor check is sound; coordinate with C24 (star becomes an <a>) and C38 (both rewrite the dropped reason nodes) in one pass.

value: Y — Verified: 'reason merged into F1' references a label rendered nowhere — an unresolvable cross-reference in a rigor-themed audit is a direct trust break, and visible IDs make findings citable in issues.

title: Make the rail ✴ a permalink and style :target so jumps visibly land
problem: Findings have stable anchors and the scorecard deep-links to them (line 822), but the star is an inert aria-hidden span (line 906) and grep confirms no :target rule exists — a reader who wants to cite one finding has to open devtools, and after a smooth-scroll jump the landed card looks identical to every neighbor (with scroll-margin-top:5rem the previous finding's tail is visible above, so there is no confirmation of which card answered the click). For a report meant to be cited in issues and PRs, deep-linking is core product behavior.
recommendation: Render the ✴ as a self-link (<a href="#f-{id}">) with an aria-label — the finding's sigil becomes its permalink, zero new decoration — and add a quiet persistent :target state in the existing vocabulary (title takes accent while the hash matches), plus an optional one-shot wash already neutralized by the reduced-motion block (line 410). Clicking the star also puts the copyable URL in the address bar.
amendments: Keep the persistent :target title accent; omit the one-shot background wash animation — the quiet state is confirmation enough.
Build the star-anchor alongside C08's rail-head ID so the rail is touched once.
reasons: taste: Y — For an artifact cited in issues and PRs, the sigil-as-permalink is function disguised as the existing glyph, and a quiet :target state confirms the jump; coordinate the star markup with C08.

risk: Y — The anchor keeps the .f-star class so existing rail styles apply, :target plus the motion-gated wash uses only existing tokens (accent verifies 4.83:1 dark / 6.39:1 light on the title), and it must merge with C08's railhead wrapper in one edit.
value: Y — Citing individual findings in issues and PRs is core product behavior for this artifact, and landing a jump with zero confirmation of which card answered is a real wayfinding failure.

title: Deep links land off-target: re-scroll to the URL fragment after render and after fonts load
problem: Content is built at DOMContentLoaded (lines 1124–1125), so when someone opens report.html#f-F4 the target does not exist at parse time; and even after the browser's fragment retry, EB Garamond arrives from the CDN and reflows the entire serif page — on a document this long the anchor shifts substantially, parking the reader mid-way through the wrong finding.
recommendation: After init() finishes, and again when document.fonts.ready resolves, re-scroll to location.hash if present. Three lines of vanilla JS, fully self-contained.
evidence: final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 1124–1125 (render at DOMContentLoaded), Google Fonts link line 11, line 90 (smooth scroll)
reasons: taste: Y — Deep links that park the reader mid-way through the wrong finding break the product behavior C24 establishes; three invisible lines of vanilla JS, fully in-budget.
risk: Y — Three lines, guarded on location.hash, self-contained; the only edge (a late fonts.ready re-scroll) targets the same fragment the reader navigated to, which is harmless.
value: Y — Deep links that park readers mid-wrong-finding after the Garamond reflow break the share moment entirely — and C24's permalinks are only as good as where they land.

title: The page dead-ends: add a quiet wayfinding line (back to top · findings index) above the footer credit
problem: This is one very long flat flow and there is NO route back up from anywhere: grep finds no '#top', no back-to-top, and no link to #scorecard-sec (line 477) anywhere. A reader who jumps from the scorecard to finding 7 has only a long manual scroll back; the footer ends on a single attribution sentence (renderFooter, lines 1032–1052).
recommendation: End the page with a quiet mono wayfinding row above the colophon: '↑ Back to top · Findings, by principle' — Fira Code, uppercase, tx-2, accent on hover. '#top' is a spec-defined fragment that scrolls to document top with no matching id needed; #scorecard-sec already exists. Preferred over a floating ↑ button (extra fixed chrome, off-brand) — the footer pair is the restrained shippable fix.
evidence: before-footer-dark.png; final-rail-stars-v3.html lines 477, 1032–1052; grep for '#top'/'scrollTo' returns nothing
reasons: taste: Y — A very long flat flow with no route back up is a real dead-end, and the footer pair is the restrained answer — correctly rejecting the floating button that would have been off-brand; coordinate placement with C51 (nav above credit, colophon below).
risk: Y — '#top' is spec-defined with no id required, #scorecard-sec exists at line 477, and the additive footer row touches no invariant.
value: Y — A 12,000px document with provably no route back up is a genuine wayfinding gap, and the footer pair is the restrained fix versus floating chrome.
